per_turn_leaks.student_message: Worsening dyspnea with a productive cough — that combination raises concern. You mentioned fever. With fever and productive cough, have you had any night sweats or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been having some pretty bad night sweats - I mean, I'm talking about feeling really hot and uncomfortable at night for the past four nights. It's really scary, to be honest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Night sweats with fever and productive cough — that's a classic infectious triad. With respiratory infections, pleuritic involvement is common. Have you had any chest pain, especially when breathing deeply or coughing?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've been experiencing sharp right-sided chest pain when I take deep breaths or cough. It started a few days ago and has been getting worse.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Right-sided pleuritic pain fits with a lower lobe process. You mentioned allergic rhinitis — allergies and asthma often co-occur. Have you ever had wheezing episodes before this illness? Any history of asthma?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've actually had recurring wheezing episodes since I was a kid - they'd happen 2-3 times a year when I got colds or exerted myself too much. They were never formally diagnosed.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your mother having asthma strengthens the genetic link. Speaking of family history — any other respiratory or serious conditions in the family?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: With this level of illness — fever, productive cough, night sweats, chest pain — your body is fighting hard. How has your appetite been? Any weight changes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I haven't had much of an appetite over the past few days - everything tastes bland and I just don't feel like eating. As for weight changes, I've actually lost a bit of weight since this all started.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Your partner having a cold recently could be related to your initial infection that then progressed. Having a cat is worth noting too — for future reference regarding allergens and your likely asthma.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: That makes sense, I guess. I didn't think about how much time I spend around kids at work or how that might be contributing to my getting sick. And yeah, it's definitely possible that my partner's cold was what started all of this - but I'm still worried about the chest pain and night sweats.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Never smoked — good, that's one less complication. How about alcohol?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been feeling really overwhelmed by this cough and breathing stuff. It's like my body is fighting something big and I'm not sure what it is or how to make it stop. The night sweats are definitely scaring me - I just want some answers so I can start feeling better.
